ops.title: Tool-result-anchored corrections: split turn at carrier
ops.body:
# Tool-result-anchored corrections: split the anchor turn at the carrier

Corrections that arrive as tool-result-only user events ŌĆö a rejected `ExitPlanMode`
(plan review), an inline review comment ŌĆö do **not** open a new turn. cc-transcript's
`capture_window` therefore puts the material being corrected (the submitted plan, the
reviewed assistant prose) in `window.trigger` (the anchor's own turn), not in
`window.before`. Any consumer that keeps only `before` and discards `trigger` exports
a positive row whose context is a single empty assistant message.

## The failure this caused (fixed in commit de3f9bda325b)

- 82 accepted positives in the live ledger carried empty context (52 plan_review,
  30 review_comment, 45 sessions).
- v001's materialized training data was **92.6% positive on the exact empty-context
  surface** (1,111 pos / 89 neg; 35.9% of all its positive examples) ŌĆö the watcher
  literally learned "empty context ŌćÆ steer", producing its fire-on-nothing flooring
  pathology in production and on eval.

## The rules

1. **One capture seam.** `cc_steer/capture.py::capture_anchored_window` is the single
   codepath: it runs `capture_window`, then ŌĆö only when the anchor is a `UserEvent`
   mid-turn (`position_in` > 0, a tool-result-only carrier) ŌĆö splits the anchor's turn
   at the carrier. The pre-anchor prefix (plan text, reviewed prose) joins
   `window.before`; the carrier onward becomes `window.trigger` with role forced to
   `user` so `context_turns()`/`messages()` exclude it. Every consumer (live scan,
   mirror scan, sidecar, and therefore export) must go through this seam ŌĆö never add
   a second capture path.
2. **Never emit empty positives.** Every export view (watcher, live, KTO, DPO, SFT)
   validates the prompt it actually emits and raises `EmptyWatcherPrompt`; accrual
   quarantines empty-context candidates at the single insert choke point
   (`store.record_file_scan`); `freeze_eval` raises `EmptyEvalContext` naming
   offending rows. Emptiness checks must test substance **beneath role markup**
   (`<assistant>\n` is not content).
3. **Rebuild = re-detect.** Historical repair (`rebuild-context` migration) re-runs
   the real detection codepath (`detect(scrub_events(events))`) per transcript copy
   and joins fresh candidates to stored rows by `dedup_key` ŌĆö never re-derive windows
   with hand-rolled parameters (a raw `capture_anchored_window(before=6)` loses
   detector-specific boundaries like `clamped_before`/plan-reentry). Copy selection
   is content-maximal (not newest-mtime); gate-sample repair is comparison-driven
   against the row's current window; stored rows are `UPDATE`d explicitly (the insert
   path is `INSERT OR IGNORE` and will never refresh them).
4. **Watch the label boundary.** The single worst regression class here is the
   correction carrier leaking INTO the captured context (label leakage into the model
   input). Tests must pin the anchor boundary by refs ŌĆö assert the anchor event is
   absent from `before` refs and present in `trigger` refs ŌĆö not just by rendered
   string content.
ops.when: before modifying context capture, anchor handling, or integrating detectors with user feedback
